c:Annotation.c:AnnotationText: Eurostat
Eurostat
Eurostat

s:Dataflow.c:Name: HICP - monthly data (index)
HVPI - Monatliche Daten (Index)
IPCH - données mensuelles (index)
s:Dataflow.c:Description: The Harmonised Index of Consumer Prices (HICP) measures price changes of various consumer goods and services over time (inflation). The index measures price changes in relation to a base year. Data are available with 2 decimals for the aggregates and 1 or 2 decimals for countries.

Der Harmonisierte Verbraucherpreisindex (HVPI) misst Preisänderungen bei verschiedenen Konsumgütern und Dienstleistungen im Zeitverlauf (Inflation). Der Index misst Preisänderungen gegenüber einem Basisjahr. Die Daten sind mit 2 Dezimalstellen für die Aggregate und 1 oder 2 Dezimalstellen für die Länder verfügbar.

L’indice des prix à la consommation harmonisé (IPCH) mesure l’évolution des prix de divers biens et services de consommation au fil du temps (inflation). L'indice mesure les variations de prix par rapport à une année de base. Les données sont disponibles avec 2 décimales pour les agrégats et 1 ou 2 décimales pour les pays.

s:Codelist.c:Name: Classification of individual consumption by purpose (COICOP)
Klassifikation der Verwendungszwecke des Individualverbrauchs (COICOP)
Classification des fonctions de consommation des ménages (COICOP)
s:Codelist.c:Description: The classification of individual consumption by purpose, abbreviated as COICOP, is a classification developed by the United Nations Statistics Division to classify and analyse individual consumption expenditures incurred by households, non-profit institutions serving households and general government according to their purpose. It includes categories such as clothing and footwear, housing, water, electricity, gas and other fuels.

c:Annotation.c:AnnotationText: Combined code list, contains standard codes and codes not associated with the concept of the code list
Eurostat Standard Code Lists

The geographical standard code list provides codes on geographical entities at the level of aggregates, countries, regions and organizations; the labels are available in EN, DE and FR and they are in line with the EU Interinstitutional Style Guide. The work is based on various international sources: Interinstitutional Style Guide, ISO 3166 country codes (3166-1 alpha-2), UN standard country and area codes classification. The structure follows the UN standard country or area code classification (M49) and adopts in most of the cases the proposed geographical order. In case of conflict, the most appropriate solution in the interest of the ESS has been chosen. The country codes correspond to the ISO 3166 classification with the exception of: United Kingdom where the code UK was adopted (instead of GB) and Greece where the code EL was adopted (instead of GR) in order to comply with the Interinstitutional Style Guide. The presentation of the code list has been enhanced with additional information on the composition of the aggregates and on the dependency status of various geographical entities (mainly overseas territories). ISO 3166-1-alpha-2 - ISO 3166 International Standard for country codes; NUTS
Not derived
